mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances following a FEDFUNDS increase.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration, reducing consumer spending expectations and triggering short-term equity sell-offs within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 4.0% threshold, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based demand contraction, leading to corporate earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST yield positive 60-day forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 1.0 percent month-over-month during high inflation periods signals capacity bottlenecks, prompting anticipated monetary tightening and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth >1.0% during CPIAUCSL >3.0% yields positive 20-day forward returns in >50% of observations, or the mechanism shows no predictive power in high-inflation subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above 2.0 percent annualized confirms economic expansion, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 > 2.0% correlates with negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of release windows, or the signal's Information Coefficient drops below 0.02 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stagnant federal funds rate creates negative real yields, eroding purchasing power and triggering equity market declines over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases while FEDFUNDS stagnates, yet 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of cases, or the joint effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls growth exceeding 300,000 in a single month signals an overheating labor market, increasing wage inflation pressures and prompting anticipatory equity sell-offs over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS > 300k yields positive 60-day forward returns in >55% of instances, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for prior month revisions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index expansion above its 12-month moving average reflects strengthening manufacturing output, improving corporate profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO above its 12-month moving average correlates with negative 20-day forward returns in >50% of months, or the signal fails to achieve a positive Sharpe ratio in out-of-sample testing.
